tweets.title: Jio's 1,600-Satellite LEO Constellation Cleared by IN-SPACe, Designed to Support Military Payloads India's space regulator, IN-SPACe, officially approved Reliance Jio's proposal to deploy around 1,600 low-Earth orbit (LEO) satellites. Jointly evaluated with ISRO and the Department of Telecommunications, the homegrown system boasts a massive capacity of up to 5 terabits per second (Tbps) and is designed to host defense payloads for national security.
tweets.content:
Jio's 1,600-Satellite LEO Constellation Cleared by IN-SPACe, Designed to Support Military Payloads

India's space regulator, IN-SPACe, officially approved Reliance Jio's proposal to deploy around 1,600 low-Earth orbit (LEO) satellites. Jointly evaluated with ISRO and the Department of Telecommunications, the homegrown system boasts a massive capacity of up to 5 terabits per second (Tbps) and is designed to host defense payloads for national security.

tweets.title: BREAKING: PoK Neither "Azad" Nor "Disputed" But "Occupied": Local Leader At Rally Amid ongoing unrest in Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir (PoJK), protest leader Sardar Aman Khan declared at a massive rally in Rawalakot that the region is "neither Azad nor disputed, but occupied." The protests have continued for over 40 days, with demonstrators alleging shortages of food and medicines, internet blackouts, and excessive use of force by Pakistani authorities. India's Ministry of External Affairs has accused Pakistan of systematic exploitation, denial of fundamental rights, and a violent crackdown on civilians in PoJK. The situation remains tense, with protests continuing across the region.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: PoK Neither "Azad" Nor "Disputed" But "Occupied": Local Leader At Rally

Amid ongoing unrest in Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir (PoJK), protest leader Sardar Aman Khan declared at a massive rally in Rawalakot that the region is "neither Azad nor disputed, but occupied."

The protests have continued for over 40 days, with demonstrators alleging shortages of food and medicines, internet blackouts, and excessive use of force by Pakistani authorities.

India's Ministry of External Affairs has accused Pakistan of systematic exploitation, denial of fundamental rights, and a violent crackdown on civilians in PoJK.

The situation remains tense, with protests continuing across the region.

tweets.title: BREAKING: Infiltration Bid Foiled Along LoC in J&K's Rajouri Indian Army troops opened fire after detecting suspicious movement near the Line of Control (LoC) in the Tarkundi sector of Rajouri on Friday night, triggering an exchange of small-arms fire with Pakistani positions. The firing reportedly lasted around 90 minutes. No casualties have been reported. A search operation is underway to determine whether any infiltration attempt succeeded, while security forces have placed the area under heightened surveillance.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Infiltration Bid Foiled Along LoC in J&K's Rajouri

Indian Army troops opened fire after detecting suspicious movement near the Line of Control (LoC) in the Tarkundi sector of Rajouri on Friday night, triggering an exchange of small-arms fire with Pakistani positions.

The firing reportedly lasted around 90 minutes. No casualties have been reported.

A search operation is underway to determine whether any infiltration attempt succeeded, while security forces have placed the area under heightened surveillance.

BREAKING: Gujarat ATS has foiled an active time bomb plot, arresting 5 Jaish-e-Mohammed (JeM) operatives across the state.

Here is a breakdown of the ongoing operation:

The Plot: One of the arrested operatives had advanced explosive knowledge and was captured right before completing a live time bomb. A previous attempt by the group to test a crude explosive had failed.

The Network: These arrests bring the total number of captured operatives to 18. The sleeper cell, operating under "Darul Islam Gujarat Jaish-e-Mohammed," was actively targeting vulnerable local youth for recruitment.

The Backing: Interrogations revealed the module was receiving direct financial support and instructions from handlers based in Pakistan.

The Evidence: Authorities have seized over 250 radicalized digital files, technical manuals, and translated texts used by the module.

Central and state intelligence agencies are currently conducting multi-state searches to map the financial pathways and recover any hidden weapon caches.
